model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Duke enters this Final Four matchup as the consensus #1 team in the country (KenPom #1, Torvik #1, AdjEM +39.17) with a meaningful ~5.6-point efficiency advantage over Houston (KenPom #5, AdjEM +33.55), anchored by the nation's best defense (AdjDE 88.7) that is marginally superior to Houston's elite 4th-ranked unit (AdjDE 91.0), giving the Blue Devils an edge on both ends of the floor. The betting market opened Duke as a 5.5-point favorite with an extremely low total of 135.5, reflecting Houston's grind-it-out defensive identity under Kelvin Sampson that will slow possessions and limit Duke's offensive opportunities, while also confirming broad consensus on Duke's overall superiority. Duke continues to operate without starting point guard Caleb Foster (fractured foot, season-ending), a genuine personnel concern, but Maliq Brown is expected to play through his shoulder injury and Cameron Boozer anchors an elite two-way roster that has won multiple tournament games without Foster. Houston's credible upset path runs through pace control and defensive execution — the same formula that stunned Duke in the 2025 Final Four — but Duke's superior efficiency margin on both ends, deeper talent pool, and the market's consistent lean make the Blue Devils the right side in what projects as a competitive, low-scoring semifinal.

reasoningStep.title: Duke vs. Houston Final Four Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke holds a ~5.6-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage (KenPom #1 vs. #5) and the nation's best defense (#1 AdjDE 88.7 vs. Houston's #4 AdjDE 91.0). The betting market confirms Duke -5.5 with an ultra-low O/U of 135.5 reflecting Houston's pace-control identity. Duke is missing Caleb Foster (fractured foot, out for season) but Maliq Brown returns for the Final Four. Houston's 4th-ranked defense is the great equalizer but Duke's superior two-way profile and efficiency gap tip the pick to the Blue Devils.
evidence: Duke: KenPom #1, AdjEM +39.17, AdjOE 127.9 (#4 nationally), AdjDE 88.7 (#1 nationally). Houston: KenPom #5, AdjEM +33.55, AdjOE 124.6 (#16 nationally), AdjDE 91.0 (#4 nationally). Efficiency gap of ~5.6 points favors Duke on both ends of the floor.

Betting market: Duke -5.5 favorite, O/U 135.5 — an extremely low total reflecting Houston's defensive identity and Kelvin Sampson's pace-control system, while confirming market consensus on Duke's superiority.

Duke injury context: Caleb Foster (fractured right foot) remains out for the season; Maliq Brown (dislocated shoulder) expects to play in the Final Four and is targeting a bigger role per ESPN reporting.

Historical context: Houston stunned Duke in the 2025 Final Four per ESPN feature story, giving Houston revenge motivation. SBNation's 2026 bracket prediction explicitly picks Duke to beat Houston 'to get revenge for last year's Final Four matchup.'

No significant Houston injury concerns reported heading into the Final Four. Kelvin Sampson's defensive system (4th nationally, AdjDE 91.0) is specifically built to neutralize high-powered offenses, representing Houston's primary upset path.

result.text: Duke vs. Houston odds: Current line, win probability, final score prediction, how to watch NCAA Tournament - On3 Skip to main content Mobile Menu Pro News NFL NFL News NFL Draft News NASCAR NBA NBA News NBA Draft News Culture Sports Betting About On3 Join Now Login Duke vs. Houston odds: Current line, win probability, final score prediction, how to watch NCAA Tournament by: Grant Grubbs 03/30/25 grant_grubbs_ Read In App Not every story ends with a Cinderella at the ball. On April 5, 1-seed Duke and 1-seed Houston will square off in the Final Four inside the Alamodome in San Antonio, Texas. Duke punched its ticket to the Final Four with a stunning 85-65 blowout win over Alabama . All eyes are on freshman Cooper Flagg , who is a frontrunner for National Player of the Year and is projected to be the No. 1 pick in the upcoming NBA Draft. Alas, the Blue Devils aren’t the only ones with elite talent. Houston looked dominant against the Volunteers on Sunday, holding Tennessee to a mere 15 points in the first half. In turn, FanDuel published the odds for Duke-Houston before the buzzer even sounded in the Cougars’ Elite Eight showdown. CLICK HERE: Bet $5, Get $200 in Bonus Bets If Your Bet Wins from Fan Duel! Odds for Duke vs. Houston According to FanDuel , Duke is a 5.5-point favorite against Houston. Additionally, the Blue Devils boast -230 odds on the money line. The over/under for the game is set at just 135.5 points. For reference, Duke is averaging 82.7 points per game in comparison to Houston’s 72.2 points per outing. Final score prediction for Duke vs. Houston KenPom Predicted final score : 68-66 KenPom Win probability (for Duke): 57% KenPom is a predictive system for college basketball. It’s designed to show how strong a team is at a given time without taking factors like injuries into account. KenPom is slightly less confident in Duke than Las Vegas. The system is only favoring the Blue Devils by two points. How to watch Duke vs. Houston Time: 8:49 p.m. ET, April 5 Channel: CBS Location: Alamodome — San Antonio, TX 1-seed Duke and Houston will meet on Saturday, April 5, in the Alamodome in San Antonio, Texas, for a highly-anticipated Final Four matchup. The winner will advance to the National Championship. The Blue Devils are no strangers to the big game. Duke has appeared in the National Championship 11 times and won it five times, most recently in 2015. Meanwhile, Houston has only ever made two National Championship appearances and never won the title. Gambling Problem? C

result.text: Duke vs. Houston odds: Current line, win probability, final score prediction, how to watch NCAA Tournament - On3 Skip to main content Mobile Menu Pro News NFL NFL News NFL Draft News NASCAR NBA NBA News NBA Draft News Culture Sports Betting About On3 Join Now Login Duke vs. Houston odds: Current line, win probability, final score prediction, how to watch NCAA Tournament by: Grant Grubbs 03/30/25 grant_grubbs_ Read In App Not every story ends with a Cinderella at the ball. On April 5, 1-seed Duke and 1-seed Houston will square off in the Final Four inside the Alamodome in San Antonio, Texas. Duke punched its ticket to the Final Four with a stunning 85-65 blowout win over Alabama . All eyes are on freshman Cooper Flagg , who is a frontrunner for National Player of the Year and is projected to be the No. 1 pick in the upcoming NBA Draft. Alas, the Blue Devils aren’t the only ones with elite talent. Houston looked dominant against the Volunteers on Sunday, holding Tennessee to a mere 15 points in the first half. In turn, FanDuel published the odds for Duke-Houston before the buzzer even sounded in the Cougars’ Elite Eight showdown. CLICK HERE: Bet $5, Get $200 in Bonus Bets If Your Bet Wins from Fan Duel! Odds for Duke vs. Houston According to FanDuel , Duke is a 5.5-point favorite against Houston. Additionally, the Blue Devils boast -230 odds on the money line. The over/under for the game is set at just 135.5 points. For reference, Duke is averaging 82.7 points per game in comparison to Houston’s 72.2 points per outing. Final score prediction for Duke vs. Houston KenPom Predicted final score : 68-66 KenPom Win probability (for Duke): 57% KenPom is a predictive system for college basketball. It’s designed to show how strong a team is at a given time without taking factors like injuries into account. KenPom is slightly less confident in Duke than Las Vegas. The system is only favoring the Blue Devils by two points. How to watch Duke vs. Houston Time: 8:49 p.m. ET, April 5 Channel: CBS Location: Alamodome — San Antonio, TX 1-seed Duke and Houston will meet on Saturday, April 5, in the Alamodome in San Antonio, Texas, for a highly-anticipated Final Four matchup. The winner will advance to the National Championship. The Blue Devils are no strangers to the big game. Duke has appeared in the National Championship 11 times and won it five times, most recently in 2015. Meanwhile, Houston has only ever made two National Championship appearances and never won the title. Gambling Problem? C

modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"f4-1","winnerId":"duke","confidence":0.67,"rationale":"Duke enters this Final Four matchup as the consensus #1 team in the country (KenPom #1, Torvik #1, AdjEM +39.17) with a meaningful ~5.6-point efficiency advantage over Houston (KenPom #5, AdjEM +33.55), anchored by the nation's best defense (AdjDE 88.7) that is marginally superior to Houston's elite 4th-ranked unit (AdjDE 91.0), giving the Blue Devils an edge on both ends of the floor. The betting market opened Duke as a 5.5-point favorite with an extremely low total of 135.5, reflecting Houston's grind-it-out defensive identity under Kelvin Sampson that will slow possessions and limit Duke's offensive opportunities, while also confirming broad consensus on Duke's overall superiority. Duke continues to operate without starting point guard Caleb Foster (fractured foot, season-ending), a genuine personnel concern, but Maliq Brown is expected to play through his shoulder injury and Cameron Boozer anchors an elite two-way roster that has won multiple tournament games without Foster. Houston's credible upset path runs through pace control and defensive execution — the same formula that stunned Duke in the 2025 Final Four — but Duke's superior efficiency margin on both ends, deeper talent pool, and the market's consistent lean make the Blue Devils the right side in what projects as a competitive, low-scoring semifinal."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"f4-1-analysis","title":"Duke vs. Houston Final Four Matchup Analysis","summary":"Duke holds a ~5.6-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage (KenPom #1 vs. #5) and the nation's best defense (#1 AdjDE 88.7 vs. Houston's #4 AdjDE 91.0). Duke is missing Caleb Foster (fractured foot, out for season) but Maliq Brown returns for the Final Four.
